etymology_text: Coined in 1754 by Horace Walpole from "Serendip" + "-ity" after the Persian fairy tale "The Three Princes of Serendip."

glosses: The faculty of making fortunate discoveries by accident.
An instance of such a fortunate discovery.
examples: Landing on the perfect dataset felt like serendipity.

etymology_text: From Middle English hath, from Old English hæfþ (“has”), third-person singular of habban (“to have”).

glosses: Archaic third-person singular simple present indicative of have.
examples: He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
